seoTitle: Create an Email Template in GoHighLevel: Guide
metaDescription: Build a reusable email template in GoHighLevel to save time on campaigns, newsletters, and automated workflow emails.
intro: Creating a reusable email template in GoHighLevel saves time whenever you need to send a campaign, newsletter, or automated workflow email with consistent branding. This overview covers the general steps for building and saving a template for future use.

steps.heading: Open the email template builder
steps.body: Navigate to the section of GoHighLevel where email templates are created and managed.

steps.heading: Design your template layout
steps.body: Add sections, images, and text blocks to build the structure of your email.

steps.heading: Apply your branding
steps.body: Incorporate your logo, brand colors, and consistent styling so the template reflects your business identity.

steps.heading: Save the template for reuse
steps.body: Save your finished design as a template so it can be applied to future campaigns and workflow emails.

steps.heading: Use the template in campaigns and workflows
steps.body: Apply the saved template when building new email campaigns or automated workflow messages.

faq.question: Can I reuse the same template across multiple campaigns?
faq.answer: Yes, saving a template is specifically meant to let you reuse it across future campaigns and workflow emails.

faq.question: Do I need design experience to build a template?
faq.answer: GoHighLevel's builder tools are designed to let you create templates without needing professional design experience.

faq.question: Can I include my branding in the template?
faq.answer: Yes, applying your logo, colors, and consistent styling is part of building a branded template.

faq.question: Can templates be used in automated workflows?
faq.answer: Yes, saved templates can be applied to both manual campaigns and automated workflow emails.

faq.question: Who would benefit from creating email templates?
faq.answer: Agencies, marketers, and business owners who send regular campaigns or automated emails and want consistent branding.
